name: couch
explanation: CouchDB exposes a REST API on the well-known port 5984. The existing auto_conf.yaml templates the server URL as http://%%host%%:5984. The check fetches the server root for version detection then walks `/_node/*/_stats`, `/_active_tasks`, and per-database stats. CouchDB 2.x+ admin endpoints typically require basic auth (admin party is dev-only), so realistic deployments need credentials, but the version-probing root endpoint and unauthenticated read paths can confirm a CouchDB server is on the wire. Custom (not generic) because the integration probes multiple HTTP paths and the upstream supports two distinct API shapes (1.x vs 2.x cluster).
notes: Pattern E variant: auth-optional in spec but production deployments will require admin credentials for /_stats endpoints. Unauthenticated probe of `/` (welcome JSON with version) suffices to identify CouchDB; full metric collection falls through to credential discovery.
